title: What Every BODY Is Saying
author: Joe Navarro
subtopics: body language, nonverbal cues, reading people
subline: An ex-FBI agent's guide to speed-reading people.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- A single human silhouette (head + shoulders), in profile, looking left. Tiny red markers and labels point to 5 body cues: feet, hands, eyes, lips, shoulders. -->
  <g transform="translate(800 1200)" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif">
    <!-- The head + neck + shoulder silhouette -->
    <g fill="none" stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="2">
      <!-- Head -->
      <circle cx="0" cy="-300" r="120"/>
      <!-- Neck -->
      <path d="M -50 -200 L -50 -120 L 50 -120 L 50 -200"/>
      <!-- Shoulders + chest -->
      <path d="M -200 -120 L -200 0 L -160 80 L -100 80 L -100 0 L 100 0 L 100 80 L 160 80 L 200 0 L 200 -120 L 200 -80 Q 100 -60 0 -60 Q -100 -60 -200 -80 Z"/>
      <!-- Eye (just one, in profile) -->
      <circle cx="-50" cy="-310" r="6" fill="#ffffff"/>
      <!-- Lips -->
      <path d="M -90 -240 L -60 -240 L -60 -220 L -90 -220 Z" fill="#ffffff"/>
    </g>
    <!-- The 5 RED markers, each with a small label -->
    <g font-size="13" font-weight="500" fill="#fc1c46" letter-spacing="0.18em">
      <g>
        <circle cx="-200" cy="0" r="6" fill="#fc1c46"/>
        <line x1="-200" y1="0" x2="-360" y2="-40" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="1"/>
        <text x="-380" y="-40" text-anchor="end">SHOULDER</text>
      </g>
      <g>
        <circle cx="-160" cy="80" r="6" fill="#fc1c46"/>
        <line x1="-160" y1="80" x2="-380" y2="120" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="1"/>
        <text x="-400" y="120" text-anchor="end">HAND</text>
      </g>
      <g>
        <circle cx="-50" cy="-310" r="6" fill="#fc1c46"/>
        <line x1="-50" y1="-310" x2="280" y2="-380" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="1"/>
        <text x="300" y="-380" text-anchor="start">EYE</text>
      </g>
      <g>
        <circle cx="-75" cy="-230" r="6" fill="#fc1c46"/>
        <line x1="-75" y1="-230" x2="280" y2="-220" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="1"/>
        <text x="300" y="-220" text-anchor="start">LIP</text>
      </g>
      <g>
        <circle cx="200" cy="-80" r="6" fill="#fc1c46"/>
        <line x1="200" y1="-80" x2="380" y2="-20" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="1"/>
        <text x="400" y="-20" text-anchor="start">EAR</text>
      </g>
    </g>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: A single human silhouette in profile with 5 RED markers pointing to body cues (shoulder, hand, eye, lip, ear) — the five nonverbal tells the book decodes. 220px whisper title at the bottom — the diagram is the cover.
